_embedding_model_comment: Switched from qwen3-embedding:8b (7.5 GB VRAM, 91% saturation on RTX 4070 Laptop) to nomic-embed-text:v1.5 (~270 MB VRAM). Frees ~7.2 GB VRAM, eliminates RAM<->VRAM swap thrash on embed calls, and is ~30x faster per chunk on this card. Run 'ollama pull nomic-embed-text:v1.5' once before first context-load. To revert, set back to qwen3-embedding:8b and bump chunk-size/k-* knobs back up.
_section_gemini: Google Gemini API key. Surfaced at the top level so any future Gemini-direct tool (text/vision) can read it through config_loader.py without having to dig into acpx.agents. The same key is ALSO injected into the gemini ACP child via acpx.agents.gemini.env so that spawning the gemini CLI through acp_spawn JustWorks.
web_search_tool_description: A tool for searching the internet for current events, real-time information, or facts you do not know.
_chunk_size_comment: REDUCED to 2000 (was 3000) - Still captures full methods/classes; halves per-chunk embed latency vs 3000
_chunk_overlap_comment: REDUCED to 300 (was 800) - 2.7x less duplicated work per file with negligible recall loss
_max_chunks_per_file_comment: REDUCED to 20 (was 50) - Caps the chunk explosion on bloat files like boot.js (2.4 MB)
_use_mmr_comment: DISABLED - Standard similarity is better for finding all relevant code pieces
_k_vector_comment: REDUCED to 40 (was 100) - Still deep recall, 60% less re-ranking work
_fetch_k_comment: REDUCED to 120 (was 300) - Candidate pool 3x smaller, same precision after fusion
_enable_bm25_comment: ENABLED - Critical for keyword matching (function names, etc.)
_k_bm25_comment: REDUCED to 40 (was 100) - Match k_vector for balance
_k_fused_comment: REDUCED to 60 (was 150) - Still saturates the LLM context budget
_max_doc_chars_comment: REDUCED to 100k (was 250k) - boot.js (2.4 MB) and bundle.js (494 KB) get truncated cleanly instead of dominating the index
_max_context_chars_comment: REDUCED to 200k (was 450k) - Still fills a 128k-token context comfortably; less LLM-side rerank work

retrieval_strategy._enable_multi_stage_comment: DISABLED - Use standard RRF (Vector + BM25) for better code recall

performance._max_workers_comment: REDUCED to 6 (was 12) - 12 concurrent embed requests triggered thermal spikes on the laptop GPU; 6 keeps the GPU saturated without forcing throttle

_section_kali: Kalier / MCP-Kali-Server bridge. 'kali_server_url' is the base URL of the Flask API (server.py) running on your Kali box — Tlamatini IS the client (the embedded replacement for Claude Desktop's client.py). The wrapped chat_agent_kalier tool auto-injects this as the default server_url on every run, so plain prompts ('scan 10.0.0.5 and give me a report') work without ever repeating the URL. Default http://127.0.0.1:5000 works when Kali runs in WSL2 with localhost forwarding (or you SSH-tunnel the port); otherwise set it to http://<KALI_LAN_IP>:5000 via Config -> URLs in the navbar. The LLM may still override it per-call by passing server_url=...
_section_stm32: STM32er / STM32 Template Project MCP bridge (https://github.com/XAIHT/STM32TemplateProjectMCP). ZERO-CONFIG by default: leave 'stm32_mcp_server_script' EMPTY and STM32er auto-bootstraps — it downloads the MCP from 'stm32_mcp_repo_url' into 'stm32_mcp_install_dir' (empty = a per-user LOCALAPPDATA/Tlamatini cache), pip-installs its deps (mcp, pyserial) and validates, then spawns and drives the stdio server. The user only installs STM32CubeIDE + Tlamatini. Set 'stm32_mcp_server_script' to an absolute mcp/stm32_mcp_server.py ONLY to point at an existing local checkout (an on-disk path wins and skips bootstrap). 'stm32_mcp_python' is the Python used to RUN the server + install its deps (empty = reuse Tlamatini's Python). 'stm32_template_dir' / 'stm32_ide_root' map to the server's STM32_TEMPLATE_DIR / STM32_IDE_ROOT env. The wrapped chat_agent_stm32er tool auto-injects all of these on every run so firmware prompts never repeat them; the LLM may override any per-call by naming it.
_section_acpx: ACPX Runtime + Skills (added 2026-04-29). The whole 'acpx' block is OPTIONAL — when missing, every value below is the safe default applied automatically by agent/acpx/config.py::load_acpx_config. Documenting the keys here makes the surface discoverable.
_acpx_doc_url: See ACPX.md at the repo root and docs/claude/acpx.md (when present) for the full contract.

acpx._acpx_cwd_comment: Default working directory for ACP child processes when no per-spawn cwd is given. Empty string = use the Django process cwd at runtime.
acpx._acpx_stateDir_comment: Where AcpSession transcripts and per-session JSON records are written. Empty string = <app-base>/.tlamatini/acpx-state/ (kept under the installation directory to avoid writes to restricted user-profile folders on corporate machines).
acpx._acpx_probeAgent_comment: agent_id used by acp_doctor's --version probe. Empty string = first resolvable agent in the registry.
acpx._acpx_permissionMode_comment: One of 'approve-reads' (default; reads auto-approved, writes need a prompt), 'deny-all' (lockdown; even acp_spawn is blocked), 'approve-all' (FLAGGED DANGEROUS; auto-approves every action including writes/shell/network). Mirrors OpenClaw's ACPX_PERMISSION_MODES vocabulary exactly.
acpx._acpx_nonInteractivePermissions_comment: What to do when an action needs a prompt but no operator can answer (Multi-Turn unattended runs). 'deny' = action denied, run continues. 'fail' = whole spawn/skill fails hard.
acpx._acpx_timeoutSeconds_comment: Per-turn timeout for the embedded runtime (acp_send / initial spawn drain). Default 120s; bump for slow CLIs like Gemini cold-start.
acpx._acpx_pluginToolsMcpBridge_comment: When true, inject a built-in MCP server into ACP children that exposes Tlamatini @tools to the child. OFF by default — opting a less-trusted runtime into our tool surface is a deliberate security choice.
acpx._acpx_openClawToolsMcpBridge_comment: Reserved for forward compatibility with OpenClaw's openClaw-tools bridge. OFF by default.
acpx._acpx_strictWindowsCmdWrapper_comment: Legacy compatibility field. Accepted and logged as ignored; the current windows_spawn module handles .cmd/.bat resolution itself.
acpx._acpx_mcpServers_comment: Optional named MCP servers to inject into ACP child sessions at spawn time. Empty by default. Each entry: { command: 'python', args: ['-m', '...'], env: {...} }.
acpx._acpx_agents_comment: Per-agent_id command overrides. Empty = use the built-in registry (claude/cursor/codex/copilot/gemini/qwen/pi/droid/iflow/kilocode/kimi/kiro/opencode/tlamatini). Override when the binary lives outside PATH. Each entry can carry an optional 'env' map injected at spawn time.

_section_acpx_skills: Skills are loaded from agent/skills_pkg/<dir>/SKILL.md. The catalog is self-discovering; nothing in config.json controls it. Run `python manage.py acpx_lint` to validate the catalog.
_section_acpx_tools: ACPX exposes 7 LangChain @tools to the unified agent (acp_spawn / acp_send / acp_kill / acp_doctor / list_acp_agents / invoke_skill / list_skills). Each is a row in the Tool table seeded by migration 0071_acpx_skills and is independently toggleable through the existing tools dialog UI.
